tips.title: The Ice Trick
tips.content: Grab an ice cube and hold it in your hand until it melts. The cold shock resets your nervous system and breaks the craving cycle.

tips.title: 4-7-8 Breathing
tips.content: Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 7 seconds, exhale for 8 seconds. Repeat 3 times. This activates your parasympathetic nervous system.

tips.title: Drink Cold Water
tips.content: Sip ice-cold water slowly. The sensation helps redirect your focus and the cold can suppress the urge temporarily.

tips.title: Quick Exercise
tips.content: Do 10 jumping jacks or a quick sprint in place. Physical activity releases endorphins that fight cravings.

tips.title: Count Backwards
tips.content: Start at 100 and count backwards by 7s. This requires focus and breaks the mental loop of the craving.

tips.title: The Wave Visualization
tips.content: Imagine your craving as a wave. It's high now, but waves always crash and recede. In 3 minutes, this will pass.

tips.title: Box Breathing
tips.content: Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 4, breathe out for 4, hold for 4. Repeat until the craving passes.

tips.title: Name Your Dragon
tips.content: Give your craving a name. 'There goes Fred again, wanting a cigarette.' Naming it reduces its power over you.

tips.title: Call Your Buddy
tips.content: Call a friend or family member. Tell them you're having a craving. Just 5 minutes of connection can reset your brain.

tips.title: Step Outside
tips.content: Go outside and take a 5-minute walk. Change your environment completely. Fresh air clears your mind.

tips.title: Chew Gum
tips.content: Keep sugar-free gum handy. The oral fixation needs something to do. Chew aggressively.

tips.title: Text Someone
tips.content: Text someone: 'Having a craving right now, can you remind me why I'm doing this?'

Morning Coffee: Try drinking coffee with your non-dominant hand. Break the muscle memory!
After finishing your coffee, immediately brush your teeth or wash your hands.
替代方案: Keep cinnamon sticks nearby to chew after coffee.
Driving: Keep your car smoke-free: Put a Air Freshener and keep snacks nearby.
Roll down the window for fresh air when craving hits.
Play a podcast or music to keep your mind occupied.
Work Stress: Take 5 deep breaths before reaching for a cigarette.
Step away from your desk for a 2-minute stretch break.
Keep a stress ball or fidget toy at your desk.
Social: It's okay to step away from the crowd when craving hits.
Tell your friends you're quitting - they'll support you!
Keep your hands busy with a drink or snack.
Alcohol: Limit alcohol in early days - it lowers impulse control.
Alternate alcoholic drinks with water or soda.
Plan your exit strategy before going to events with alcohol.
After Meal: Immediately after eating, brush your teeth or chew gum.
Take a walk after meals instead of sitting.
Boredom: Keep a list of quick activities: call a friend, stretch, organize something.
The boredom is temporary - ride the wave!
Break the routine: take a different route, try something new.
Phone Break: When scrolling triggers cravings, put your phone down for 5 minutes.
Keep your phone away from your usual smoking spots.
Use app blockers during high-craving times.
Evening Relax: Create a new evening routine that doesn't include smoking.
Keep yourself busy in the first 30 minutes after dinner.
Try herbal tea or a warm drink as a replacement.

morningWisdom: Trigger Alert: If you usually smoke with coffee, try drinking it with your non-dominant hand today. Break the muscle memory!
Pro Tip: After your morning coffee, brush your teeth immediately. The fresh taste makes smoking less appealing!
💡 Smart Insight: Cravings typically last only 3-5 minutes. Set a timer and ride it out!
Today's Focus: Identify your triggers. When a craving hits, ask yourself 'What triggered this?'
validation: The craving is a wave. It peaks at 3 minutes and then it must subside. You're at the peak right now. Breathe.
Cravings are your brain healing. This discomfort means your body is recovering. Feel the feeling and let it pass.
You're stronger than this moment. This craving will pass in just 3 minutes. You've got this.
Every craving you resist is proof that you're in control. This feeling is temporary, your freedom is forever.
hype: If you give in now, you reset your {days}-day streak to zero. Don't let a 5-minute craving steal {days} days of work!
You're ${money} richer today because of your decision. Don't throw that away for a momentary urge.
Your lungs are healing right now. Every minute without a cigarette makes you stronger. Keep going!
Think about how proud you'll be tomorrow when you wake up still smoke-free. That's worth fighting for.
